item.title: Five villagers injured as sand lifters open fire
item.description: At least five villagers sustained bullet injuries as sand lifters opened fire after being resisted by local people while lifting sand from the river Meghna at Charpalong village under Nabinagar upazila in Brahmanbaria....

item.title: Human hauler driver beaten to death at Jatrabari
item.description: A human hauler driver was beaten to death allegedly in a dispute over trip serial numbers at Jatrabari Chowrasta in the capital’s Jatrabari area on Friday afternoon....

item.title: FAMILY CARD PROGRAMME: Social welfare ministry asked to frame method
item.description: The cabinet committee on providing family card to marginalised and low-income people at a meeting on Sunday asked the social welfare ministry to prepare the criteria for selecting recipients of the card within a couple of days....

item.title: Ex-DMP chief Habibur 10 others charged with Jatrabari murder
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 on Sunday indicted 11 people, including former Dhaka Metropolitan Police Commissioner Habibur Rahman, in a case over the murder of Imam Hasan Taim during the July uprising....Public speaking training

item.title: Serov Academy of Fine Arts holds special class for children
item.description: The Serov Academy of Fine Arts organised a special ‘Amar Ekushey’ class and creative programme to inspire children with the spirit of the Language Movement, marking the Martyrs’ Day and International Mother Language Day, on Green Road in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.description: An associate professor of Dhaka University’s Institute of Business Administration, ATM Zakaria Khan, died while undergoing treatment in Marie Curie West Midlands Hospice at Birmingham in the United Kingdom on ​​Wednesday....

item.description: Today is the fifth anniversary of death of writer, activist, journalist and columnist Syed Abul Maksud....

item.title: Trump hikes US global tariff rate to 15pc
item.description: President Donald Trump raised the global duty on imports into the United States to 15 per cent on Saturday, doubling down on his promise to maintain his aggressive tariff policy a day after the Supreme Court ruled much of it illegal....

item.title: Amar Ekushey Book Fair to be launched on Feb 26
item.description: The opening of Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026 has been rescheduled once again for February 26. Prime minister Tarique Rahman will inaugurate the annual book fair on the Bangla Academy premises on the day......

item.title: BNP leader shot at in Dhaka
item.description: A ward-level leader of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party sustained bullet injury in his hand as unidentified miscreants opened fire on him in the capital’s Panthapath area Sunday evening......Political party merchandise

item.title: No third party can sway Dhaka-Beijing ties: envoy
item.description: Chinese ambassador to Bangladesh Yao Wen said that no external pressure or third party would be able to influence China-Bangladesh relations, reaffirming Beijing’s firm support for strengthening bilateral cooperation under the new government....

item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Sunday ordered newspaper advertisements to notify deposed prime minister Sheikh Hasina, former home minister Asaduzzaman Khan Kamal, and four former senior officials of Dhaka Metropolitan Police to......

item.title: Yunus returns to old workplace after 18 months
item.description: Professor Muhammad Yunus returned to his institution on Sunday after serving as the head of the immediate-past interim government for 18 months....

item.title: Pak deputy PM talks with Bangladesh FM over phone
item.description: Seeking stronger cooperation, deputy prime minister and foreign minister of Pakistan Mohammad Ishaq Dar on Sunday held a telephone conversation with Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rehman, felicitating him on his recent appointment....Cultural exchange programs

item.title: Pakistan’s FM calls Bangladesh counterpart, eyes broader collaboration
item.description: Pakistan's deputy prime minister and foreign minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar on Sunday held a telephone conversation with Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman, congratulating him on his recent appointment and reaffirming commitment to stronger bilateral ties......Cultural exchange programs

item.title: Govt clears 1,006 ‘political’ cases for withdrawal
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government has decided to withdraw politically motivated cases filed during the 17-year rule of the ousted Awami League regime....

item.title: JS likely to go into 1st session on March 12
item.description: The first session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad is likely to begin on March 12, with the election of the speaker and deputy speaker scheduled to take place during the sitting....

item.title: Rethinking education pipeline
item.description: HISTORY suggests that higher education has never been static. It has shifted shape in response to industrial revolutions, scientific discovery and changes in the organisation of labour. Each major economic transition has forced universities to rethink what they teach and how they teach it. Today, we find ourselves in the midst of another upheaval — arguably deeper......

item.title: Upper house: what thou art for
item.description: NEARLY 40 per cent of the countries have adopted bicameral parliamentary system mainly with a view to imposing checks and balances on the majority party’s practice of overwhelming power, ensure regional representation in terms of ethnicity of population and the largeness of the area forming the states......Public speaking training

item.title: Tariffs, taxes and rule of law in global economy
item.description: THE recent US Supreme Court decision striking down the use of emergency powers to impose sweeping tariffs is being reported as a legal setback for president Donald Trump. That is a narrow reading. The ruling is about who controls the allocation of economic resources in a modern capitalist democracy — an individual executive or representative institutions?......

item.title: Soil, salinity and survival
item.description: CLIMATE change is commonly described as a long-term shift in average weather patterns, including temperature, rainfall and atmospheric humidity. That definition, while accurate, understates the scale of transformation underway. Climate change is not merely altering seasonal rhythms; it is reshaping the ecological foundations upon which human societies depend......

item.title: Tough action must follow to stop mob attacks
item.description: THE 18 months under the interim administration, after the fall of the Awami League government, were described by many as a period of ‘mob rule.’ Rights group Ain O Salish Kendra reported that at least 259 people had been killed and 313 injured in 413 incidents of mob violence in August 2024–January 2026. It also reported that typical cases of mob assaults, usually......

item.title: Ramadan markets strain the poor again
item.description: GOODS prices have remained high. They have either marked a sharp increase in view of Ramadan, the month of fasting for Muslims, which began on February 19, or remained almost static after having reached highs days, or even weeks, ago. The price increases have either kept many from buying essential goods, especially the ones commonly used to cook food items to......

item.title: Logical demands of teachers, employees to be met gradually: Milon
item.description: Education and primary and mass education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Sunday said that the government would gradually meet the logical demands of the teachers and employees....

item.description: The Dhaka Women and Children Repression Prevention Tribunal-4 on Sunday sentenced four people to life-term imprisonment for raping a woman on a boat in the Buriganga River in the capital September 2009....

item.description: Leaders of Workers Party of Bangladesh on Sunday at a discussion in Dhaka called on Bangladesh Nationalist Party to rule the country by restoring spirit of the 1971 Bangladesh liberation war....Cultural exchange programs

item.description: A senior Hamas official said on Sunday that the Palestinian Islamist movement was in the final phase of selecting a new leader, with two prominent figures competing for the position....

item.description: Russia fired scores of missiles and drones at targets across Ukraine on Sunday, crashing into energy and rail infrastructure and residential buildings, just two days before the fourth anniversary of Moscow’s all-out attack....Political party merchandise

item.description: Iranian students gathered for fresh pro- and anti-government rallies Sunday commemorating those killed in recent protests, as fears loomed of renewed conflict with the United States over the country’s nuclear programme....

item.title: Barca back top of La Liga with Levante win
item.description: Barcelona cruised to a 3-0 victory over Levante to reclaim pole position in La Liga on Sunday. After Real Madrid fell to a 2-1 defeat at Osasuna on Saturday, Hansi Flick's side moved a point clear of their bitter rivals with a comfortable victory at Camp Nou against the team in 19th. ...

item.title: Arsenal's Eze sinks Spurs again, Liverpool late show floors Forest

item.description: Five youths were arrested after local people had besieged the Adabar police station, protesting at extortion and alleged attack on an embroidery factory in the capital’s Mansurabad area....

item.description: Short-term money market transactions declined in January 2026 as banks reduced interbank borrowing and instead parked excess funds with the central bank, signalling cautious liquidity management under a tight monetary stance......
